title: 5. Google Doc Outline - Wrap Up & Next Steps

blocks.content: [5. Google Doc Outline - Wrap Up & Next Steps]
(Instructor Note: Use this outline to create the follow-up Google Doc/Email to be distributed to attendees after the session.)

items: **Copy:** "Congratulations! You have successfully mastered the L100 installation of Google Antigravity in a secure corporate environment. You’ve bypassed the IT roadblocks, established a secure workspace using the AGV Easy Install manager, and successfully verified your agent's execution abilities. 'No install, no magic' – and you've just unlocked the magic."
[ ] Interactive Installation (Official Binary method) is complete.
[ ] Signed into AGV using a personal gMail account.
[ ] Agent successfully wrote and executed the hello_agv.py test script.

blocks.content:
**3. What's Next in the AGV Learning Track?
**

items: **AGV-200 (The Pilot's Interface):** We will dive into navigating Mission Control, manual verification loops, and building your first app.
**AGV-300 (Orchestrated Ops):** We will introduce Jules integration, multi-surface workflows, and web scraping agents.
**AGV-400 (Autonomous SDLC):** We will scale up to enterprise rules with background configurations (gemini.md) and MCP server integrations.

**Call to Action:** "To help us tailor the L200 and L300 sessions to your specific day-to-day workflow, please complete the attached **Persona Survey**. Let us know if you are focused on frontend prototyping, backend database architecture, or infrastructure deployment so we can route you to the correct follow-up track."
